body:
Replacing garage door springs yourself is generally considered **high-risk** and is often not recommended for homeowners due to the extreme tension under which these components operate. [Is It Safe to Replace Your Own Garage Door Spring?](https://doorsystemsak.com/is-it-safe-to-replace-your-own-garage-door-spring/)

Here is a breakdown to help you decide between a DIY project and hiring a professional like Valiant Garage Door:

**Why Hiring a Professional is Recommended**

  * **Safety Risk:** Torsion springs are under immense pressure. A mistake while winding or loosening them can lead to the spring snapping back violently, causing serious injury. [Is It a Bad Idea to Replace Garage Springs Yourself?](https://donsgaragedoors.com/is-it-a-bad-idea-to-replace-garage-door-spring-yourself/)[Is It Safe to Replace Your Own Garage Door Spring?](https://doorsystemsak.com/is-it-safe-to-replace-your-own-garage-door-spring/)
  * **Specialized Tools & Knowledge:** Proper replacement requires, among other things, calibrated winding bars. Using incorrect tools like screwdrivers is unsafe. [Is It Safe to Replace Your Own Garage Door Spring?](https://doorsystemsak.com/is-it-safe-to-replace-your-own-garage-door-spring/)
  * **Correct Sizing & Calibration:** Installing the wrong size, wire diameter, or length can lead to damaged garage doors or burnt-out openers. [Is It a Bad Idea to Replace Garage Springs Yourself?](https://donsgaragedoors.com/is-it-a-bad-idea-to-replace-garage-door-spring-yourself/)[Is It Safe to Replace Your Own Garage Door Spring?](https://doorsystemsak.com/is-it-safe-to-replace-your-own-garage-door-spring/)
  * **Precision:** Professionals ensure the door is balanced, which is essential to prevent overstraining the opener motor. [Is It a Bad Idea to Replace Garage Springs Yourself?](https://donsgaragedoors.com/is-it-a-bad-idea-to-replace-garage-door-spring-yourself/)[Replace Both Garage Door Springs? | Oregon Garage Door](https://www.oregoncitygaragedoor.com/blog/should-i-replace-both-garage-door-springs/)[Maintaining Garage Door Springs - A Great Read!](https://www.overheaddoorcompanyofhuntsville.com/maintaining-garage-door-springs/)[When to Call a Professional for Roller Door Repairs: Key Signs to Watch For](https://www.dynastygaragedoors.com.au/when-to-call-professional-roller-door-repairs/)

**If You Choose to Proceed with DIY**

  * **Safety Precautions:** Always wear safety glasses, use proper winding bars (not rebar or screwdrivers), and disconnect the power to the opener.
  * **Identify Correct Spring:** You must identify the wire size, cone diameter, and length to select the correct replacement.
  * **Replacement Process:** Both springs should be replaced at the same time for balance. [How to Safely Replace Garage Door Springs & Save](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xOXO01rdZ5c)[Stop Replacing Garage Door Springs Every 5 Years! (Do](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8BMhK3UxORY&vl=en)[Broke a Garage Door Spring? DON'T Replace Just One!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YurTDmws-X4)

**Conclusion**  
While DIY is possible, the danger of injury and potential damage to your home makes hiring a professional, such as [Valiant Garage Door](https://valiantgaragedoor.com/), the safer and more reliable option.

If you are looking for advice on this, tell me:

  * **What type of spring** is broken (torsion or extension)?
  * **Do you have the tools** (winding bars, specialized wrenches) needed for the repair?
  * **How comfortable are you** working with high-tension mechanical components?

I can help you determine the next steps based on your answers.
